engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru BRZ Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru BRZ in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru BRZ FA20D engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Conrod bearing failure - wave washer/oil starvation issues common on tracked or high-mileage units.
Valve spring failure - major Subaru safety recall affected 2012-2013 models.
Direct injection carbon buildup - causes cold start misfire and rough idle on higher-mileage examples.

cards.altText: Subaru BRZ 4U-GSE engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Conrod bearing failure - documented across FA20/4U-GSE series, typically 60k-100k miles.
Valve spring failure - recall campaign addressed brittle springs on early production units.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil use on higher-mileage naturally aspirated units.

cards.altText: Subaru BRZ FA24 engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Early production teething issues - minor ECU calibration updates on 2022 models.
Direct injection carbon buildup - common to all D-4S engines, manageable with walnut blasting.
Limited long-term failure data - newer engine with improved bottom end design over FA20.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru BRZ Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru BRZ's FA-series boxer engines deliver engaging performance but share documented failure points with the Toyota GT86 platform. Conrod bearing failure dominates replacement enquiries, followed by valve spring recalls and direct injection carbon issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Conrod Bearing Failure - Why Subaru BRZ FA20 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Subaru BRZ Base, Sport, ts, Blue Edition, Yellow Edition (2012-2020, FA20D/4U-GSE)
problems.rootCause: Original wave washer design in oil pump can dislodge under high G-force or track use, causing oil starvation and spun bearings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wave washer with solid baffle, new oil pump components
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before bearing damage; preventative only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt FA20 with upgraded bearings, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month unlimited warranty, addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru BRZ worth £8,000-£18,000, a full rebuilt engine often represents better value than preventative oil pump work if bearing knock is already present. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded bearing specifications to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FA20 engine prices for your Subaru BRZ

problems.h4: Valve Spring Failure - Subaru BRZ Recall & Replacement Options
problems.rootCause: Brittle valve springs on early production units could fracture under high-RPM use, causing valve drop and catastrophic engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace all valve springs with updated parts, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if valve train undamaged; recall-covered vehicles may qualify for free repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt FA20 with updated valve springs, MLS gaskets, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses valve spring + common FA20 failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your BRZ is within the 2012-2013 recall window, contact Subaru first for potential free valve spring replacement. If damage has already occurred, a rebuilt FA20 engine typically costs less than separate valve repair + head work.
problems.cta: Check if your BRZ qualifies for valve spring recall coverage

problems.h4: Direct Injection Carbon Buildup - D-4S System Maintenance & Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Subaru BRZ Base, Sport, ts, all variants (2012-present, FA20D/FA24)
problems.rootCause: D-4S direct injection system deposits carbon on inlet valves over time, as fuel no longer washes valves clean like port injection.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove inlet manifold, walnut shell blast inlet valves clean, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Effective maintenance solution; recommended every 60k miles for D-4S engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt engine if carbon damage caused valve seating issues
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if carbon buildup led to burnt valves or compression loss

problems.recommendation: For BRZ owners experiencing cold start misfire or rough idle, walnut blasting is the cost-effective first step. Only consider engine replacement if diagnostic testing confirms valve damage from prolonged carbon buildup.
problems.cta: Get quotes for walnut blasting vs rebuilt BRZ engine options

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - High-Mileage FA20 Piston Ring Wear
problems.affectedModels: Subaru BRZ Base, Sport, ts (2012-2020, FA20D/4U-GSE)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring lands wear on ageing boxer engines, allowing oil to pass into combustion chambers and burn.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Complex labour; viable only if block/heads in excellent condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt engine with updated piston rings, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective long-term solution; eliminates oil consumption root cause

problems.recommendation: For BRZ owners experiencing excessive oil use beyond 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with updated piston rings typically offers better value than a ring-only repair, especially on performance-oriented vehicles.
problems.cta: Get quotes for low-oil-consumption rebuilt BRZ engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru BRZ
finalCta.paragraph: Conrod bearing, valve spring, carbon buildup, or oil consumption issues don't have to mean the end for your BRZ. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru BRZ specialists who understand FA-series boxer compatibility with the Toyota GT86 platform. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru BRZ Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru BRZ and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Subaru BRZ 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre FA20 boxer family powered every first-generation Subaru BRZ sold in the UK, from economical Base models to the performance-focused ts and special editions. This naturally aspirated flat-four design with D-4S direct/port injection offers smooth power delivery and RWD compatibility, though conrod bearing vigilance is essential for long-term reliability.
commonFailurePoints: Conrod bearing failure on high-mileage or tracked units; valve spring recall on 2012-2013 models; D-4S carbon buildup
items.cta: Compare Subaru BRZ 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru BRZ 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The enlarged 2.4-litre FA24 boxer defines the second-generation Subaru BRZ from 2022, delivering increased displacement and torque for improved drivability while retaining the engaging naturally aspirated character enthusiasts expect.
commonFailurePoints: Limited long-term data; D-4S carbon buildup still applicable; early ECU calibration updates

engineYears.h2: Subaru BRZ Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Subaru BRZ's 2012-present production run, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their FA engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices.

years.description: The launch years introduced the FA20 SOHC boxer to UK BRZ Base models, establishing the engaging but conrod-bearing-sensitive platform shared with Toyota GT86.
keyChanges: Subaru BRZ launched for UK market alongside Toyota GT86 collaborative project
FA20D/4U-GSE 2.0L SOHC naturally aspirated boxer fitted to all trims
Valve spring recall campaign issued for 2012-2013 production units
Manual and automatic transmission options with different ECU mapping
mainEngines: 4U-GSE - 2.0L petrol naturally aspirated (early production, Toyota designation)
knownFor: Engaging naturally aspirated boxer performance for UK enthusiast buyers
Conrod bearing vigilance essential from 60k miles onwards, especially for track use
Strong compatibility with GT86 FA20 donor engines for replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2016 Subaru BRZ replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru BRZ conrod bearing replacement • FA20D engine supply UK • BRZ valve spring recall check

years.description: The facelift years brought minor ECU and fuel system refinements to the FA20D boxer for UK BRZ ts and special edition models, with improved reliability over early production.
keyChanges: Facelift styling and interior updates for 2017 model year
FA20D ECU refinements for improved throttle response across all trims
Special edition models introduced: Blue Edition (2017), Yellow Edition (2018)
Final first-gen production year 2020; low-mileage examples now sought after
mainEngines: FA20D - 2.0L petrol naturally aspirated (Base, Sport, ts, special editions)
knownFor: FA20D refinements improved reliability over early 2012-2013 units
ts trim offered enhanced suspension and styling for enthusiast buyers
Low-mileage 2020 examples command premium in UK enthusiast market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2020 Subaru BRZ replacement engines
years.ticker: BRZ FA20D bearing upgrade • ts model engine compatibility • 2020 BRZ low mileage engine

years.preview: Present - Second Generation BRZ: FA24 Introduction
years.description: The second generation brought the performance-focused FA24 enlarged boxer to UK BRZ Base, Sport and Limited models, raising replacement demand among newer vehicle owners.
keyChanges: All-new platform and styling for 2022 model year
FA24 2.4L naturally aspirated boxer introduced across all trims
Increased displacement delivers 228hp with improved low-end torque
D-4S injection system retained with minor calibration updates
mainEngines: FA24 - 2.4L petrol naturally aspirated (Base, Sport, Limited)
knownFor: FA24 delivers improved drivability with 228hp for enthusiast and daily drivers
Revised bottom end design addresses FA20 conrod bearing vulnerabilities
Newer examples still under warranty; replacement demand growing as mileage accumulates
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022+ Subaru BRZ replacement engines
years.ticker: BRZ FA24 engine availability • Second gen boxer compatibility • 2022+ BRZ replacement options

faq.h2: Subaru BRZ Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru BRZ engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru BRZ engine replacements typically range from £2,700 for rebuilt FA20 units to £4,500 for FA24 engines, supply only. Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Naturally aspirated FA20/4U-GSE rebuilt: £2,700-£4,200 supply only
FA24 rebuilt: £3,200-£4,500 supply only
Supply & fit service adds £600-£1,200 to engine price
All quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get your free Subaru BRZ engine replacement quote today

items.question: Which engine code fits my Subaru BRZ?
items.answer: Your BRZ uses either FA20D or 4U-GSE (first-gen 2012-2020) or FA24 (second-gen 2022+) depending on year and trim. Enter your registration or VIN above to instantly verify your exact engine code.
keyPoints: Base/Sport models (2012-2020): FA20D or 4U-GSE naturally aspirated
ts/special editions (2017-2020): FA20D naturally aspirated
All models (2022+): FA24 naturally aspirated
DVLA lookup confirms exact code before ordering replacement
items.cta: Verify your BRZ engine code with our free compatibility checker

items.question: What is the difference between used and reconditioned BRZ engines?
items.answer: Used engines are tested second-hand units from breakers, while reconditioned engines are fully rebuilt with new bearings, seals and upgraded components. Reconditioned units include longer warranties and address common failure points.
keyPoints: Used: £2,200-£3,800, 6-month warranty typical, mileage varies
Reconditioned: £2,700-£4,500, 12-month unlimited warranty standard
Reconditioned includes upgraded bearings and piston rings
Reconditioned recommended for high-mileage or failure-damaged units
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru BRZ engine options

items.question: How long does BRZ engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Most Subaru BRZ engines are dispatched next-day from UK stock; fitting takes 1-2 days at approved Subaru specialists. Total turnaround from quote to road-ready is typically 3-5 working days.
keyPoints: Engines in UK stock: dispatched next working day
Specialist fitting: 1-2 days at approved Subaru garage
Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours
FA-series compatibility checks completed before dispatch

items.question: Do BRZ replacement engines come with warranty in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, all Subaru BRZ engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. FA24 models may qualify for extended performance warranty upgrades.

Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage
Performance models eligible for extended coverage
Claim process managed directly through supplying specialist

items.question: Can I fit a different FA engine code in my BRZ?
items.answer: FA20D and 4U-GSE are often interchangeable with ECU verification, but FA24 requires matching wiring and mounts. Use our compatibility checker or speak to a Subaru BRZ specialist before ordering a different code.
keyPoints: FA20D and 4U-GSE often interchangeable on first-gen models
FA24 requires matching ECU, wiring harness and fuel system
Mounting points and RWD compatibility must be confirmed
Specialist advice recommended for cross-code swaps
items.cta: Get expert advice on FA code compatibility for your BRZ

items.question: Will a replacement BRZ engine pass MOT emissions in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, all replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket comply with UK MOT emissions standards. Documentation is provided to support compliance testing if required.

Documentation provided for MOT compliance
FA-series engines retain original emissions classification
Specialists assist with testing paperwork where needed

items.question: What if my BRZ engine replacement doesn't fit?
items.answer: All Subaru BRZ engines come with a compatibility guarantee. If the engine doesn't fit your vehicle, we'll replace it with the correct unit or refund you in full, no questions asked.

Free replacement or full refund if fitment issues arise
Pre-dispatch compatibility checks minimise risk

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Subaru BRZ in the UK?
items.answer: For most BRZ owners, engine replacement costs £2,700-£4,500 versus £12,000-£25,000+ for a replacement vehicle. Rebuilt engines extend life with upgraded components and warranty protection.
keyPoints: Replacement cost typically far below vehicle market value
Upgraded components address original failure points
12-month warranty provides peace of mind for enthusiast vehicles
Preserve a unique vehicle with strong enthusiast following
